rationale: The pair appears substantively connected: the citing study’s core question is how fast-fashion copycats affect premium-brand popularity and it runs policy counterfactuals by copycat-similarity thresholds, while the cited paper specifically studies when high- vs. moderate-similarity copycats perform differently under comparative evaluation. The citing note explicitly frames itself as extending prior copycat research, and the matched DOI confirms this exact work is in its references, so this is not safely an obvious incidental mention.
